content_of_post_to_answer: How can I have better ways of reducing anxiety?
answer: Many people try to reduce anxiety by fighting the feeling itself, but anxiety often grows when the mind treats uncertainty as a problem that must be solved immediately. A more useful shift is learning to separate real danger from imagined discomfort. The goal is not to eliminate anxious thoughts, but to stop giving every thought the authority of a warning signal. Anxiety becomes quieter when the brain learns that uncertainty can be experienced without needing constant control.
